title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Brent fell to about $78 (WTI ~$75), a three-month low, with the war premium “largely priced out” — but the move is partly trapped barrels clearing into a soft market (Asian refiners pre-booked, Chinese runs in maintenance), not just risk-off.

The pact's full text is public — as a read-out, not a signed PDF — and it buys time: relief now, with nuclear, Lebanon, enforcement and the Hormuz toll deferred to a 60-day clock. Called and resolved: the text did surface by the 18th.

The under-covered macro: Odesa's ports could lose a third of monthly grain capacity while Ukraine funds the winter grid from the same purse — and markets eye a possible October Fed hike even as oil falls.
JPMorgan pulled Claude for Hong Kong staff, after Goldman — banks becoming the compliance layer for AI geopolitics while Anthropic's Fable 5 / Mythos 5 stay dark a sixth day.

props.title: Citi marks the peace into the curve
props.subtitle: Citi Brent forecast after the US-Iran MOU · US$/bbl
props.source: Reuters, on Citi's post-MOU forecast cut (Q3/Q4 2026, 2027)

items.why: Not the signing the calendar promised — the first working session on turning a read-out into operative terms.
Restore via a US-person / trusted-partner carve-out, or harden — as more banks gate access by jurisdiction?
Citi sees flows largely normal by mid-to-late July; the desk watches transits, insurance and mine-clearance, not the communiqué.
Whether the one-third capacity-at-risk estimate becomes an observed export fall — and feeds food prices.
Whether the media fight moves from Washington to the states with a filed complaint.

items.what: The IEA's June report says the deal sent oil to its lowest since March, but prices had already softened on a surge in Gulf exports and weaker demand — and it cut 2026 refinery runs further, the supply glut hiding inside the peace.
The FAO Food Price Index was broadly stable in May even as cereal quotations rose — the calm baseline a Black Sea port shock would land on if Odesa's capacity keeps slipping.

The wording that tripped JPMorgan was licensing, not law: Anthropic's terms reportedly exclude the Greater China region, so the bank read the contract strictly and pulled the tool — compliance by clause, not by statute.
